cue: How do you warn someone not to meddle in a marriage?

es.text: Entre marido y mujer, nadie se debe meter
es.literal: between husband and wife, nobody should get involved
es.meaning: Stay out of other people's marriages.
es.example: No opines sobre su pelea: entre marido y mujer, nadie se debe meter.
es.exampleTranslation: Don't weigh in on their fight — never come between a couple.

it.text: Tra moglie e marito non mettere il dito
it.literal: between wife and husband do not put your finger
it.meaning: Do not interfere in a couple's business.
it.example: Lascia perdere: tra moglie e marito non mettere il dito.
it.exampleTranslation: Leave it alone — don't come between a couple.

bridge.note: A rare double: both proverbs rhyme, and both rhyme on the act of meddling — Spanish pairs mujer with meter, Italian pairs marito with dito. Spanish states the rule impersonally, as law. Italian makes it physical and second-person: keep your finger out. And the two languages order the couple in opposite directions, husband first in Spanish, wife first in Italian.

interference.es_to_it: The Italian is an imperative aimed at you, not a general principle — and the fixed order is moglie before marito.
interference.it_to_es: The Spanish has no finger and no imperative; it legislates rather than warns.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian proverb list 2026-07-18 (learner-site discovery; all glosses and examples authored fresh). ES counterpart chosen by Claude from recall — UNVERIFIED. Both sides rhyme — the strongest form-mirrors-form pairing in the bank so far.
